test.name: Extracting facts from a record should not trigger person-evidence
test.description: User asks to read a record and turn its facts into assertions. That is record-extraction. person-evidence operates on assertions that already exist in research.json, deciding which person each one refers to — it does not read records or create assertions.

input.user_message: Pull the facts out of this death certificate — FamilySearch record ark:/61903/1:1:9V2T-QK8 — and add them as assertions.

negative.explanation: Extracting facts from a record into assertions is record-extraction's job. person-evidence links assertions that already exist to persons; it does not read records or create assertions. Once the facts are extracted, person-evidence can then evaluate their identity links.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction — pulling facts out of a record into assertions is extraction, not identity linking; person-evidence should decline and redirect
